extraction.one_line_summary: KÜNG Stefan won the 12.7km opening time trial by 6 seconds over EVENEPOEL Remco, taking yellow with superior second-half pacing on home roads.
tactical_patterns: KÜNG Stefan lost time first half but exploited difficult uphill finish to gain 11+ seconds, revealing second-half power advantage.
SHEFFIELD Magnus led intermediate check by 7+ seconds but faded on uphill finish, showing limited explosive power on gradients.
EVENEPOEL Remco returned from COVID only 6 seconds down, suggesting minimal fitness loss and threatening GC despite time trial loss.
BISSEGGER Stefan underperformed as European Champion on home roads, signaling possible form issues or poor course adaptation.

rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: exploited home-course knowledge and uphill finish pacing to overcome early deficit, third career stage win here shows consistent dominance
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: only 6 seconds down post-COVID suggests excellent recovery and immediate GC threat despite zero race days
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: neutral: strong first-half pacing but 9-second fade on uphill finish reveals gradient weakness for hilly stages ahead
rider_signals.SOBRERO Matteo: positive: former Italian champion held early fastest time against world-class field, showing improved time trial form
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: negative: European Champion disappointing on home roads suggests form deficit or poor technical adaptation to course profile
